rows.why_a: RFO +13%, AHE +24% but flattered by Malawi & market returns; underlying ~7-9% ex-Malawi.
rows.why_b: Momentum NHE +41%, Sanlam/Discovery double-digit operating-profit growth on cleaner drivers.

rows.why_a: VNB -52% to R850m; margin 1.2%, BELOW the 2-3% target after persistency-basis strengthening.
rows.why_b: Sanlam margin 2.22% (VNB -18%); OMU starts furthest below its own range.

rows.why_a: RoNAV 15.2% just into 15-17% (14.0-14.5% ex-Malawi); ROEV 7.8%; RoGEV 4.1%.
rows.why_b: Momentum ROE 21.2%; Sanlam adj RoGEV ~15.4% annualised above hurdle - cleaner, higher returns.

rows.why_a: DPS +8% (in 6-9% range); R3bn buyback live (R1.3bn done); discretionary capital ~doubled to R6.1bn.
rows.why_b: Peers progressive dividends; OMU active buyback + strong remittances a genuine relative strength.

rows.dimension: Strategic / growth optionality (bank ramp, cost programme)
rows.why_a: OM Bank live, deposits/customers ahead of plan, breakeven FY2028; R2.5bn cost programme started.
rows.why_b: Peers (Discovery Bank, SanlamAllianz) more advanced but OMU's self-help has larger reset upside.

rows.why_a: Shareholder solvency 162% (155-185% target) though -2,000bps yoy on market moves; Level-1 B-BBEE.
rows.why_b: SA composites broadly well-capitalised; OMU sits comfortably mid-range, on par with peers.

avg_note_a: OMU: a value-reset story - capital return and bank optionality offset weak VNB and market-flattered earnings.
avg_note_b: Peers: cleaner, higher-quality earnings momentum today, especially Momentum and Sanlam.
